essence: Ensures manufacturing systems remain reliable by using AI to predict failures, optimise uptime, and maintain autonomous production environments.

identity.title: AI Reliability Engineer
identity.summary: Uses AI, machine‑health analytics, and predictive modelling to maximise equipment reliability, reduce downtime, and support autonomous manufacturing operations.

salary.notes: Engineers in aerospace, robotics, or fully autonomous smart‑factory environments can exceed £145k-£190k+.

qualifications: Experience in reliability engineering, maintenance, or AI‑driven optimisation
Knowledge of predictive analytics, sensors, and machine‑health monitoring
Skills in ML models for failure prediction and reliability forecasting
Understanding of CNC, robotics, and Industry 4.0 systems

lifestyle.description: AI reliability engineers work in aerospace, robotics, automotive, and Industry 4.0 environments. They ensure equipment remains reliable by using AI to predict failures, optimise uptime, and support autonomous production. The role suits individuals who enjoy diagnostics, optimisation, and high‑tech engineering.

habits: Builds AI‑driven reliability and uptime models
Monitors machine‑health data in real time
Collaborates with maintenance, AI, and production teams
Ensures stable, reliable operation in autonomous workflows
